title: Procedure explanation — colonoscopy consent
candidateInstructions: You are an FY2 in gastroenterology outpatients. Mr Paul Graham, a 55-year-old civil servant, has been referred for a colonoscopy following rectal bleeding and a change in bowel habit. He has never had the procedure before and is very anxious. Please explain the procedure and consent him.
examinerNotes: Colonoscopy consent station. Candidate must explain: bowel prep (laxative the day before), sedation options (IV sedation/GTN), flexible camera through the back passage, air insufflation, can take biopsies, polyp removal. Risks: perforation (1:1000), bleeding, missed polyps, incomplete exam, reaction to sedation. Candidate must mention cancer screening context.

patient.name: Paul Graham
patient.presentingComplaint: Referred for colonoscopy for rectal bleeding and bowel changes
patient.reasonForAttendance: I've been told I need a camera examination of my bowel. I'm very worried.

hpi.onset: 6 weeks of rectal bleeding and change in bowel habit
hpi.character: Fresh blood mixed with stool, looser stools

systemsReview.gastrointestinal: Rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit

patient.emotionalState: Very anxious about cancer and the procedure. Will ask 'is this cancer?' and 'will it hurt?'
patient.communicationStyle: Anxious, asks direct questions. Needs clear explanation. Will ask about cancer diagnosis possibility.
hiddenConcerns: My mother died of bowel cancer — is this what I have?
Do I have to be awake for it?
Will I need to take time off work?
doNotVolunteer: Family history of bowel cancer — only if asked
Aspirin — only if asked about blood thinners

primaryDiagnosis: Rectal bleeding with change in bowel habit — 2-week wait colonoscopy for possible colorectal carcinoma

dataGathering.detailedExpectation: What does patient understand about why they need colonoscopy
Aspirin — must be stopped before polypectomy

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Laxative solution day before, clear fluids, empty bowel needed
Flexible camera, back passage, air to open up, can take samples/remove polyps, sedation option

clinicalManagement.criterion: Explains main risks — perforation and bleeding
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Perforation 1:1000, bleeding (higher if polyp removed), missed polyps, incomplete exam
Intravenous sedation option (not general anaesthetic), may feel some discomfort/bloating

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Responds to cancer question appropriately — explains colonoscopy is to investigate
Patient agrees, opportunity for questions given

references.source: NICE NG12: Suspected cancer — recognition and referral

learningObjectives: Address anxiety about cancer diagnosis sensitively
